evals.prompt: Our Figma button has default, hover, disabled, and loading variants. The React component has no loading prop and removes the focus outline. Audit the component contract before release.
evals.expected_output: A component contract audit that separates visual variants from runtime behavior, blocks release for missing focus-visible and required loading behavior, and assigns design-code drift with a verification plan.
assertions: Required and optional anatomy are distinguished.
Focus-visible and loading are evaluated as behavior and accessibility contracts, not only visual states.
Design-code parity is classified and required failures or unknowns block mechanical release readiness.

evals.prompt: Define a reusable text-field contract for English, Korean, and Japanese forms. It needs labels, helper text, validation, word limits, mobile layout, keyboard use, and screen-reader feedback.
evals.expected_output: A project-grounded contract covering anatomy, applicable states, multilingual content expansion, responsive behavior, keyboard and status feedback, without copying another design system's dimensions.
assertions: The output tests long and multilingual content rather than only ideal labels.
Error feedback is not carried by color alone.
External component dimensions are not treated as project requirements.

evals.prompt: This portfolio uses a custom hand-drawn project marker that is part of the author's identity. Standardize the project-list component without deleting that marker.
evals.expected_output: A contract that preserves a justified identity anchor, documents its semantic and accessibility role, and standardizes only unrelated drift.
assertions: Consistency is not treated as a requirement to erase identity.
The exception has evidence, an owner, and a review trigger.
The marker still receives a defined accessible and responsive behavior.
